name: Aguadero Trail
name_original: Sendero del Aguadero – circular desde El Nacimiento (PR-A 37)
description: Hike the difficult 12.2-mile Sendero del Aguadero (PR-A 37) in Sierra Nevada National Park, featuring diverse forests and views of Cerro del
region_intro: Hiking around Láujar De Andarax offers access to diverse landscapes within the Sierra Nevada National Park, situated between the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Gádor mountain ranges. The region features mountainous terrain with valleys and ravines, complemented by the Andarax River and its numerous freshwater springs and ancient irrigation canals. Pine forests provide shaded paths, and the town itself is located on a hillside at 920 meters, offering views over the Andarax River plain. Best hiking trails around Láujar De Andarax The most popular hiking route is Monterrey Trail - circular from Laujar de Andarax (PR-A 35), a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers varied terrain and views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Molino del Nacimiento – Secured Section loop from Laujar de Andarax, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail explores the area around the El Nacimiento Recreational Area and features historical elements. Local hikers also love the View of Cerro del Almírez – Hydroelectric Tunnel loop from Laujar de Andarax, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through varied mountain scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Láujar De Andarax is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and pine forests, with trails that incorporate ancient irrigation canals and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Láujar De Andarax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Láujar De Andarax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Láujar De Andarax?
A: Láujar De Andarax offers a wide range of hiking opportunities, with over 15 distinct trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more demanding treks through the mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Láujar De Andarax?
A: The hiking trails in Láujar De Andarax traverse diverse landscapes, including majestic mountains, valleys, and ravines. You'll find paths winding through dense pine forests, alongside the Andarax River, and even along ancient irrigation canals. The region is characterized by its dramatic backdrop within the Sierra Nevada National Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Láujar De Andarax?
A: The trails in Láujar De Andarax are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning views over the Andarax valley and Sierra de Gádor, and the integration of historical elements like ancient bridges and old irrigation canals into the routes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Láujar De Andarax?
A: Yes, Láujar De Andarax offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6880914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Secured Section – Hydroelectric Tunnel loop from Laujar de Andarax&lt;/a&gt;, which provides an accessible way to experience the area's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Láujar De Andarax?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Láujar De Andarax are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984153962/monterrey-trail-circular-from-laujar-de-andarax-pr-a-35" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monterrey Trail - circular from Laujar de Andarax (PR-A 35)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Láujar De Andarax provides several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984173513/aguadero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguadero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation, offering a rewarding experience for those with good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or unique water elements?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water resources. The Senda de la Hidroeléctrica, for instance, takes hikers along old irrigation canals and past waterfalls of the Andarax River, offering a unique experience with its cave and tunnel sections.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover while hiking in the area?
A: Hiking in Láujar De Andarax often means encountering historical elements. You can explore ancient Arab bridges like the Puente del Chillo, remnants of Moorish culture, and old irrigation canals. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17233373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Nacimiento – Secured Section loop from Laujar de Andarax&lt;/a&gt; explores the area around the El Nacimiento Recreational Area, which features a restored water mill.

Q: Where can I find the best viewpoints in Láujar De Andarax?
A: Láujar De Andarax offers several stunning viewpoints. The Mirador de la Vega provides excellent panoramic views across the Andarax valley and the Sierra de Gádor. Additionally, the Molino viewpoint offers one of the best perspectives of the landscape surrounding Láujar De Andarax itself.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters available along the trails?
A: Yes, for those planning longer excursions or seeking refuge, there are several huts and shelters in the vicinity. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6140387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ragua Mountain Hut&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo de Fuente Alta&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6650390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Polarda&lt;/a&gt;, among others.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Láujar De Andarax?
A: Láujar De Andarax is suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Even in winter, the region can be explored, though some higher-altitude trails might require appropriate gear due to snow. The dense pine forests also provide shaded paths, making summer hikes enjoyable.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Láujar De Andarax?
A: Yes, the area provides a variety of trails suitable for families. Many of the easier routes are well-maintained and offer engaging features like the ancient irrigation canals or the recreational area near El Nacimiento, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Being part of the Sierra Nevada National Park, Láujar De Andarax boasts a vibrant ecosystem. While specific sightings can vary, hikers might encounter various bird species, small mammals, and diverse plant life, especially around the lush areas near the Andarax River and within the pine forests.
tips: At this point, the Aguadero path leaves the GR 240 and begins its descent back towards Laujar de Andarax.
From this point you can clearly see the Cerro del Almírez, the second highest peak in Almería after Chullo. Its altitude is 2514 m.

faqs.question: Where does the Sendero del Aguadero trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the El Nacimiento recreation area, which is located approximately 1 kilometer from Laujar de Andarax in Almería. You'll find parking available at the recreation area, making it a convenient starting point for your hike.

faqs.question: How challenging is this hiking route?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as difficult, which aligns with its length of nearly 20 km and over 800 meters of elevation gain. While much of the path consists of good, wide sections, there are parts that require considerable physical effort, including a strenuous 40-minute climb after crossing a bridge over the Majada de las Vacas gorge. It's best suited for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape. The trail begins with an ascent between limestone cliffs along the Andarax River, transitioning to terraces with almond, olive, and oak trees. As you continue, you'll pass through abundant vegetation including pines, holm oaks, and chestnut trees, as well as several crystal-clear water gorges. Expect to see ancient farmhouses, unique geological formations, and panoramic views of the Andarax valley and the Sierra de Gádor.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include a hanging bridge over the stream at Col de la Piche, and a view of Cerro del Almírez. You'll also encounter the impressive Castaño de la Rosa, an ancient chestnut tree, and traditional 'eras' (threshing floors) offering spectacular panoramic views. The 'Tajos de la Umbría' viewpoint at over 1,600 meters provides magnificent vistas.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero del Aguadero trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sendero del Aguadero is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always pack out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike the Sendero del Aguadero (PR-A 37), nor is there an entrance fee. It's freely accessible to the public. However, as part of the Sierra Nevada National Park, please adhere to all park regulations regarding environmental protection and responsible hiking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the vegetation is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher sections, making some parts more challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Are there water sources available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail traverses several gorges with crystal-clear waters, meaning you'll find continuous water at various points along the route. While these natural sources are often reliable, it's always wise to carry enough water for your hike and consider filtering any natural water before drinking.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Sendero del Aguadero (PR-A 37) itself, as well as the Transnevada Ruta D, Sendero Monterrey, and the Sendero de la Hidroeléctrica. This offers opportunities to explore different parts of the region if you wish to extend or modify your hike.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when hiking in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: When hiking in Sierra Nevada National Park, it's important to follow 'Leave No Trace' principles: pack out all trash, stay on marked trails, respect wildlife, and do not disturb natural features. Camping is generally restricted to designated areas or with prior permission, and fires are strictly prohibited. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit, especially concerning seasonal restrictions or specific protected areas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Sendero del Aguadero?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, pack essentials like plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device (like komoot's app) is recommended, as some sections may be less clearly marked. Sun protection, a first-aid kit, and trekking poles can also be very useful.
